notes: RANCH's slow detective (2004) · fourteen evenings, one suspect, one kitchen. An insurance investigator reopens a thirty-year-old ferry file. Reviewers called it 'RANCH's Estuary,' and both studios hated that. Ines Rocha named it her favorite stick of 2004 · the rivalry's détente.

shelf.label_subtitle: fourteen evenings · one suspect · one kitchen
shelf.shelf_condition: immaculate · finished exactly once · the manual's verdict page has one pencil check mark, never erased
shelf.provenance_note: Olaf bought it new, finished it once, never replayed: 'you don't reread a confession.' The pencil check on the manual's verdict page is his. Tem has not asked which box it's in. Tem has looked.
shelf.cover_blurb: Albert Glass, seventy-one, retired ferry clerk, agreed to the interviews by return post. He always answers. That is the problem. From RANCH: a mystery with no chase, no corpse, and one kitchen. Bring your patience. He has more.
back_of_case: · Twelve standing questions. Ask them again. And again.
· THE LEDGER: pin any two answers against each other.
· He is always cooking. Pay attention to the menu.
'The investigation's real object is the investigator's patience.'
— Slowstick Review Quarterly, 5 boxes out of 5
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: The ex-Oneironautics founders reaching back toward the studio they left. RANCH grows the soul the schism said they'd traded away — an Estuary-shaped game about a ledger, which is the apology neither studio would say aloud.
shelf.meta_note: The drive-home theme never plays during interviews. On evening 14 it finally plays in the kitchen, very quietly, and the player realizes Glass is humming it, and has been the source all along.

lore_tokens_conditional.glass_verdict_accuse: the file closes · the kitchen goes dark
lore_tokens_conditional.glass_verdict_clear: the file closes · both of you know
lore_tokens_conditional.glass_verdict_closed: CLOSE THE LEDGER · not innocence, not guilt · settled
lore_tokens_conditional.glass_second_ledger_seen: the envelopes answer heard · consumed by Pirate Summer's bulletin board
